reason: The step 'R is a norm-limit of polynomials in S^2, so R commutes with S' tacitly uses joint (separate) continuity of multiplication in the Banach algebra End(B(H)_sa): RS=lim p_N(S^2) S=lim S p_N(S^2)=SR. This continuity is a genuine premise (it holds because End(B(H)_sa) is a Banach algebra -- cf. Kitaev line 642 'bounded linear operators form a Banach algebra') but it is neither cited nor named. Also, the node asserts a dependency only on node 1.2; but node 1.2 establishes convergence/the norm bound, not that End(B(H)_sa) is a (complete) Banach algebra in the order-unit operator norm, which is what makes the limit and the multiplication continuity legitimate. The supporting facts (completeness of B(H)_sa in order-unit norm; the induced operator norm makes End a Banach algebra) should be explicitly provenanced or carried as named dependencies.
